attacking_style: Specializes in perfectly timed solo attacks late in races, typically on climbs or finishing circuits when peloton organization breaks down. Strikes with 1-3km remaining on final ascents, exploiting hesitation in chase groups.
team_tactics: Operates with minimal team support, often working from domestique role into opportunistic breakaways. Self-sufficient raider who bridges solo to breaks rather than being prepositioned.
terrain_preference: Category 1 climbs and technical finishing circuits suit best. Excels on repeated short climbs where resilience and recovery matter. Thrives in chaotic, disorganized race situations where reading ability trumps raw power.
vulnerability: Initial difficulty on first climb passages before recovering suggests early-race struggles or slow warmup. Relies on peloton disorganization—unlikely to succeed against coordinated chase from strong teams.
key_patterns: Solo late attacks (3 observations): strikes alone with timing precision, not in groups
Exceptional race-reading in chaos: capitalizes when peloton caught or disorganized (2 observations)
Bridge specialist: solo efforts to join or create breaks mid-race (2 observations)
Resilience/recovery ability: shows unusual capacity to bounce back after struggling (2 observations)
Holds narrow gaps: wins by 6-22 seconds, sufficient power to maintain but not dominate
form_trajectory: positive: 2022 Vuelta showed evolution from pure domestique to resilient attacker, successfully defending solo efforts across multiple race types
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 5
2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 5
